user_outcome: Running `sworn version` (or `sworn --version`) reports the real version from a single embedded source for ANY build method — `go build`, `go install`, or `make` — instead of the 0.0.0-dev placeholder, while release builds can still override via ldflags.
rationale: Today the version is an ldflags-injected literal with a dev default (cmd/sworn/main.go:27 `var version = "0.0.0-dev"`; Makefile `VERSION ?= 0.1.0` -> `-X main.version`). A plain `go build`/`go install` skips ldflags and reports 0.0.0-dev (how the live /home/brad/go/bin/sworn was built). Establish one embedded source: internal/version/version.txt, embedded via go:embed (go:embed cannot reach a repo-root file from cmd/sworn, so the file lives in the version package). main.go uses it as the default and still honours an ldflags `-X main.version` override; the Makefile sources the SAME file so there is no second copy to drift.

effort_complexity.rationale: New version package (txt + go:embed), one main.go default change preserving ldflags override, one Makefile line. No runtime logic.

acceptance_criteria.text: internal/version/version.txt SHALL hold the version string (initial value "0.1.0") as the single source of truth; internal/version/version.go SHALL embed it via //go:embed and expose a Get() string that returns the trimmed value.

When the binary is built with a plain `go build ./cmd/sworn` (no ldflags) and `sworn version` is run, it SHALL print the value from internal/version/version.txt (e.g. 0.1.0), NOT 0.0.0-dev. Reachability artefact: capture `go build -o /tmp/sworn ./cmd/sworn && /tmp/sworn version`.

If the binary is built with `-ldflags "-X main.version=<X>"` (release build), then `sworn version` SHALL report <X> (the explicit override wins over the embedded default). Proven by a build with an override value.

The Makefile SHALL source the version from internal/version/version.txt (e.g. `VERSION ?= $(shell cat internal/version/version.txt)`) so `make build` and the embedded default report the same value — one source, no drift.
After the change, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./internal/version/...` SHALL pass.
